headline: Fed holds rates steady, signals two cuts remain on table for 2025
deck: Federal Reserve officials kept interest rates unchanged while reaffirming their projection for two rate reductions before year-end, citing progress on inflation but persistent labor market strength.

headline: EUR/USD holds above 1.088 as dollar rally loses momentum
deck: The euro steadied after three weeks of losses, with traders unwinding long-dollar bets ahead of next week's inflation release.

headline: China's industrial output beats forecasts as exports surge 12% in February
deck: Factory activity expanded at its fastest pace in seven months, driven by robust overseas demand for electronics and EVs.

headline: JPMorgan, Goldman post record Q1 trading revenues on volatility spike
deck: Wall Street's largest banks reported blowout results as market turbulence drove clients to hedge positions aggressively.

headline: EU regulators open formal probe into Microsoft's AI integration with Office
deck: Investigators will examine whether bundling Copilot into the productivity suite constitutes unlawful market foreclosure under the Digital Markets Act.

headline: Trump administration imposes sweeping new tariff schedule; markets digest implications
deck: The White House unveiled the most significant overhaul of import duties in decades, targeting sectors from semiconductors to pharmaceuticals.

headline: ECB divided on pace of easing as eurozone growth outlook deteriorates
deck: Minutes from the March meeting reveal sharp disagreement between hawks and doves pushing for faster cuts.

headline: UK inflation falls to 2.8%, closest to target in three years
deck: Britain's consumer price index dropped more sharply than expected, raising expectations of a Bank of England rate cut as early as May.

headline: OpenAI launches GPT-5 with unprecedented multimodal reasoning
deck: The model demonstrates a significant leap in scientific problem-solving and code generation, according to the company's evaluations.

headline: G7 agrees framework on AI governance, stops short of binding rules
deck: Finance and technology ministers endorsed voluntary principles for frontier AI development, but critics warned the measures lack enforcement mechanisms.
